frameworks.name: motivation-performance link
frameworks.one_line: Negative evaluation as a motivational signal that boosts subsequent task performance.

frameworks.name: goal-directed behavior and attention orienting
frameworks.one_line: Insula-striatum signals couple with goal/attention regions to facilitate performance.

core_question: Does receiving negative evaluation, beyond motivating people, actually enhance their performance on a subsequent risky challenge?
key_assumptions: Participants believe evaluations reflect their prior performance
fMRI BOLD connectivity reflects task-relevant coupling
manipulations: Valence of evaluation feedback (positive vs negative) from independent judges
Decision to take up a risky challenge after feedback
key_findings: Negative evaluation facilitated performance on the risky challenge
Insula and striatum represented the motivating effect of negative evaluation
Performance boost tracked functional connectivity between insula and goal-directed / attention-orienting regions
interpretations: Negative evaluation converts into performance facilitation via insula-striatum motivational coding plus insula coupling to goal and attention networks
